programs.programName: Arizona PACE (Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly)

verified.notes: PACE program is not currently operating in Arizona as of April 2026. Search results indicate Arizona has no active PACE programs[2][3]. While Arizona legislation (HB 2383) established PACE as a state option under Medicaid with a September 1, 2006 implementation deadline[1], current state and federal data confirm Arizona is listed among states without PACE programs[2][3]. No eligibility parameters, asset limits, or application phone numbers are applicable because the program does not exist in Arizona. To verify current status or inquire about future implementation, contact Arizona AHCCCS directly.

programs.programName: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)

verified.notes: Income limits from nonprofit source citing current federal guidelines; no official .gov source with exact 2026 figures in results. No age or asset tests.

programs.notes: Income limits from nonprofit source citing current federal guidelines; no official .gov source with exact 2026 figures in results. No age or asset tests.

verified.notes: Income limits based on SRP program data (200% federal poverty guideline). Annual figures converted to monthly: 1 person $30,120/12=$2,510; 2 persons $40,880/12=$3,407. Phone number is City of Phoenix Weatherization program contact. Program has no published age requirement or asset limits. Arizona Department of Housing administers statewide program; contact varies by county/service area.

programs.programName: Arizona State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)

verified.notes: Arizona SHIP is an active counseling program for Medicare beneficiaries. Phone number confirmed from official CMS and state sources. Program does not have eligibility requirements based on age, income, or assets—it provides free counseling to all Medicare-eligible individuals and their families. No specific age, income, or asset limits apply to access SHIP services.

programs.programName: Arizona Long Term Care Ombudsman Program

verified.notes: The Arizona Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program is an advocacy and complaint resolution service, not a financial assistance or eligibility program. It has no age, income, or asset requirements for residents to receive ombudsman services. The program is free and confidential. Phone number 602-542-6454 is the official state office contact per DES (Arizona Department of Economic Security). Regional area agencies on aging also provide access to ombudsman services.
